mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor income and consumption, which directly reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment rose from 9.4% to 9.8% in the 12 months ending August 2009
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC on UNRATE remains below -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are not negatively correlated with UNRATE changes at p<0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 2%, it signals that the central bank has entered an aggressive easing cycle, compressing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:By August 2009 the FOMC had held rates at 0-0.25% for several months
failure_condition: 60-day returns following the trigger are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.10) or Sharpe ratio of triggered instances is negative over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in retail sales and decrease in the unemployment rate jointly indicate strengthening consumer demand and improving labor market conditions, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases month-over-month and UNRATE decreases month-over-month for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending and employment were key concerns in late 2009
failure_condition: The dual condition occurs in fewer than 5 instances over the walk-forward period, or the conditional 20-day forward return is not greater than the unconditional mean by at least 100 bps annualized.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery period, rising housing starts signal improving household wealth and construction sector vitality, which leads investors to anticipate stronger economic growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts had fallen 70% from peak by early 2009 and were at extremely low levels
failure_condition: Rank IC of HOUST level or change is negative during periods when GDP growth has been positive for two consecutive quarters, or 20-day forward returns are not positively correlated with HOUST at p<0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in industrial production below a key threshold (index < 90) signals severe output contraction and deepening recession, which reduces corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO had fallen by over 15% year-over-year by mid-2009
failure_condition: Returns after trigger are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p>0.10) or the condition occurs in fewer than 10 distinct periods in the test set.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.2% signals that deflation fears are receding and demand is stabilizing, which improves corporate pricing power and investor sentiment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI was near zero or slightly negative in the first half of 2009, raising deflation concerns
failure_condition: Rank IC of CPIAUCSL mom change over 5-day returns is not positive and significant (p<0.10) during periods when inflation expectations were low (<2% breakeven).

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarter-over-quarter growth rate in real GDP signifies that the economy has exited contraction, which shifts equity market focus from crisis to recovery and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the new trend is priced in.
evidence_refs: fact:Q2 2009 GDP was reported in late August 2009 at -0.7% annualized, but the rate of decline was sharply smaller than Q1
failure_condition: The trigger occurs but 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 60% of instances, or the average return after trigger is less than 1% absolute.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or near zero and nonfarm payroll employment begins to increase month-over-month, the combination of maximum monetary accommodation and labor market healing creates a powerful positive impulse for equity prices, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:By September 2009, the Fed funds rate was 0-0.25% and monthly job losses were narrowing
failure_condition: The joint condition does not produce a positive average 60-day return over any two-year evaluation window, or the signal fails to outperform a simple proxy (e.g., only PAYEMS) in terms of Sharpe ratio.
